text: ňĻThis isňļ a s√Ľrah which We have revealed and made ňĻits rulingsňļ obligatory, and revealed in it clear commandments so that you may be mindful.

As for female and male fornicators, give each of them one hundred lashes, <sup foot_note=694>694</sup> and do not let pity for them make you lenient in ňĻenforcingňļ the law of Allah, if you ňĻtrulyňļ believe in Allah and the Last Day. And let a number of believers witness their punishment.

foot_notes.694: The address here is to legal authorities. Adultery has to be proven either by confession or the testimony of four reliable witnesses.

text: A male fornicator would only marry a female fornicator or idolatress. And a female fornicator would only be married to a fornicator or idolater. This is ňĻallňļ forbidden to the believers. <sup foot_note=695>695</sup>

foot_notes.695: It is reported in a Šł•ad√ģth collected by At-Tirmi‚Ī¨i that Marthad ibn Abi Marthad, a companion of the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) , asked if he could marry ‚ÄôAn√Ęq, a pagan prostitute he befriended in Mecca before he accepted Islam. So this verse was revealed. However, if someone committed a sin then later repented genuinely, they will be accepted by Allah and the rest of the believers.

text: Those who accuse chaste women ňĻof adulteryňļ and fail to produce four witnesses, give them eighty lashes ňĻeachňļ. And do not ever accept any testimony from them‚ÄĒfor they are indeed the rebellious‚ÄĒ
except those who repent afterwards and mend their ways, then surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

And those who accuse their wives ňĻof adulteryňļ but have no witness except themselves, the accuser must testify, <sup foot_note=696>696</sup> swearing four times by Allah that he is telling the truth,

foot_notes.696: In order to be spared the punishment for false accusation.

text: and a fifth oath that Allah may condemn him if he is lying.
For her to be spared the punishment, she must swear four times by Allah that he is telling a lie,
and a fifth oath that Allah may be displeased with her if he is telling the truth. <sup foot_note=697>697</sup>

foot_notes.697: This ruling is called li‚Äô√Ęn in Islamic legal system. Once the husband and the wife swear each five times, as specified in 24:6-9, the marriage is terminated forever‚ÄĒwhich means that they can never be remarried.

text: ňĻYou would have suffered,ňļ had it not been for Allah‚Äôs grace and mercy upon you, and had Allah not been Accepting of Repentance, All-Wise.

Indeed, those who came up with that ňĻoutrageousňļ slander are a group of you. Do not think this is bad for you. Rather, it is good for you. <sup foot_note=699>699</sup> They will be punished, each according to their share of the sin. As for their mastermind, <sup foot_note=700>700</sup> he will suffer a tremendous punishment.

foot_notes.699: Because now you can tell who is a true believer and who is a hypocrite.
foot_notes.700: This refers to ‚ÄôAbdull√Ęh ibn Ubai, the leader of the hypocrites in Medina.

text: If only the believing men and women had thought well of one another, when you heard this ňĻrumourňļ, and said, ‚ÄúThis is clearly ňĻan outrageousňļ slander!‚ÄĚ
Why did they not produce four witnesses? Now, since they have failed to produce witnesses, they are ňĻtrulyňļ liars in the sight of Allah.
Had it not been for Allah‚Äôs grace and mercy upon you in this world and the Hereafter, you would have certainly been touched with a tremendous punishment for what you plunged into‚ÄĒ
when you passed it from one tongue to the other, and said with your mouths what you had no knowledge of, taking it lightly while it is ňĻextremelyňļ serious in the sight of Allah.
If only you had said upon hearing it, ‚ÄúHow can we speak about such a thing! Glory be to You ňĻO Lordňļ! This is a heinous slander!‚ÄĚ
Allah forbids you from ever doing something like this again, if you are ňĻtrueňļ believers.
And Allah makes ňĻHisňļ commandments clear to you, for Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
Indeed, those who love to see indecency spread among the believers will suffer a painful punishment in this life and the Hereafter. Allah knows and you do not know.
ňĻYou would have suffered,ňļ had it not been for Allah‚Äôs grace and mercy upon you, and had Allah not been Ever Gracious, Most Merciful.

O believers! Do not follow the footsteps of Satan. Whoever follows Satan‚Äôs footsteps, then ňĻlet them know thatňļ he surely bids ňĻall toňļ immorality and wickedness. Had it not been for Allah‚Äôs grace and mercy upon you, none of you would have ever been purified. But Allah purifies whoever He wills. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

Do not let the people of virtue and affluence among you swear to suspend donations to their relatives, the needy, and the emigrants in the cause of Allah. Let them pardon and forgive. Do you not love to be forgiven by Allah? And Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful. <sup foot_note=701>701</sup>

foot_notes.701: This verse was revealed when Abu Bakr AŠĻ£-ŠĻĘidd√ģq, ‚Äô»āishah‚Äôs father, swore to end his financial assistance to his poor cousin, MisŠĻ≠aŠł• ibn Athathah, for taking part in the rumours against ‚Äô»āishah. Once the verse was revealed, Abu Bakr resumed his assistance to MisŠĻ≠aŠł•.

text: Surely those who accuse chaste, unsuspecting, <sup foot_note=702>702</sup> believing women are cursed in this life and the Hereafter. And they will suffer a tremendous punishment

foot_notes.702: Immoral thoughts never cross the minds of these chaste women and, therefore, they never expect slander.

text: on the Day their tongues, hands, and feet will testify against them for what they used to do.
On that Day, Allah will give them their just penalty in full, and they will ňĻcome toňļ know that Allah ňĻaloneňļ is the Ultimate Truth.

Wicked women are for wicked men, and wicked men are for wicked women. And virtuous women are for virtuous men, and virtuous men are for virtuous women. The virtuous are innocent of what the wicked say. They will have forgiveness and an honourable provision. <sup foot_note=703>703</sup>

foot_notes.703: This passage declares the innocence of ‚Äô»āishah, the Prophet‚Äôs wife.

text: O believers! Do not enter any house other than your own until you have asked for permission and greeted its occupants. This is best for you, so perhaps you will be mindful.
If you find no one at home, do not enter it until you have been given permission. And if you are asked to leave, then leave. That is purer for you. And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of what you do.
There is no blame on you if you enter public places <sup foot_note=704>704</sup> where there is something of benefit for you. And Allah knows what you reveal and what you conceal.
ňĻO Prophet!ňļ Tell the believing men to lower their gaze and guard their chastity. That is purer for them. Surely Allah is All-Aware of what they do.

And tell the believing women to lower their gaze and guard their chastity, and not to reveal their adornments <sup foot_note=705>705</sup> except what normally appears. <sup foot_note=706>706</sup> Let them draw their veils over their chests, and not reveal their ňĻhiddenňļ adornments <sup foot_note=707>707</sup> except to their husbands, their fathers, their fathers-in-law, their sons, their stepsons, their brothers, their brothers‚Äô sons or sisters‚Äô sons, their fellow women, those ňĻbondwomenňļ in their possession, male attendants with no desire, or children who are still unaware of women‚Äôs nakedness. Let them not stomp their feet, drawing attention to their hidden adornments. Turn to Allah in repentance all together, O believers, so that you may be successful.

foot_notes.705: i.e., hair, body shape, and underclothes.
foot_notes.706: i.e., the face, hands, outer clothes, rings, kohl, and henna.

text: Marry off the ňĻfreeňļ singles among you, as well as the righteous of your bondmen and bondwomen. If they are poor, Allah will enrich them out of His bounty. For Allah is All-Bountiful, All-Knowing.

And let those who do not have the means to marry keep themselves chaste until Allah enriches them out of His bounty. And if any of those ňĻbondspeopleňļ in your possession desires a deed of emancipation, <sup foot_note=708>708</sup> make it possible for them, if you find goodness in them. And give them some of Allah‚Äôs wealth which He has granted you. Do not force your ňĻslaveňļ girls into prostitution for your own worldly gains while they wish to remain chaste. <sup foot_note=709>709</sup> And if someone coerces them, then after such a coercion Allah is certainly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful ňĻto themňļ.

foot_notes.708: Meaning, if a slave wants to buy his or her own freedom.
foot_notes.709: Some slave-masters used to force their slave-women into prostitution before Islam. In Islam, wilful prostitution is a sin, as is forcing others into prostitution. Victims of this crime are not held accountable.

text: Indeed, We have sent down to you clear revelations, along with examples of those who had gone before you, <sup foot_note=710>710</sup> and a lesson to the God-fearing.

foot_notes.710: Before ‚Äô»āishah, Mary and Prophet Joseph had each been falsely accused of fornication, but Almighty Allah declared their innocence.

text: Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth. His light <sup foot_note=711>711</sup> is like a niche in which there is a lamp, the lamp is in a crystal, the crystal is like a shining star, lit from ňĻthe oil ofňļ a blessed olive tree, ňĻlocatedňļ neither to the east nor the west, <sup foot_note=712>712</sup> whose oil would almost glow, even without being touched by fire. Light upon light! Allah guides whoever He wills to His light. And Allah sets forth parables for humanity. For Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.

foot_notes.711: This is a metaphor for the light of guidance in the heart of the believer.
foot_notes.712: Meaning, the olive tree is wholesome because it is located in a central place, so it is hit by sunrays all day-long, and, therefore, the oil is of a premium quality.

text: ňĻThat light shinesňļ through houses ňĻof worshipňļ which Allah has ordered to be raised, and where His Name is mentioned. He is glorified there morning and evening

by men <sup foot_note=713>713</sup> who are not distracted‚ÄĒeither by buying or selling‚ÄĒfrom Allah‚Äôs remembrance, or performing prayer, or paying alms-tax. They fear a Day when hearts and eyes will tremble,
ňĻhopingňļ that Allah may reward them according to the best of their deeds, and increase them out of His grace. And Allah provides for whoever He wills without limit.

As for the disbelievers, their deeds are like a mirage in a desert, which the thirsty perceive as water, but when they approach it, they find it to be nothing. Instead, they find Allah there ňĻin the Hereafter, readyňļ to settle their account. And Allah is swift in reckoning.

Or ňĻtheir deeds areňļ like the darkness in a deep sea, covered by waves upon waves, <sup foot_note=714>714</sup> topped by ňĻdarkňļ clouds. Darkness upon darkness! If one stretches out their hand, they can hardly see it. And whoever Allah does not bless with light will have no light!

foot_notes.714: This is another scientific fact mentioned in the Quran: the existence of underwater waves, like layers.

text: Do you not see that Allah is glorified by all those in the heavens and the earth, even the birds as they soar? Each ňĻinstinctivelyňļ knows their manner of prayer and glorification. And Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all they do.
To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And to Allah is the final return.

Do you not see that Allah gently drives the clouds, then joins them together, piling them up into masses, from which you see raindrops come forth? And He sends down from the sky mountains ňĻof cloudsňļ loaded with hail, pouring it on whoever He wills and averting it from whoever He wills. The flash of the clouds‚Äô lightning nearly takes away eyesight.
Allah alternates the day and night. Surely in this is a lesson for people of insight.

And Allah has created from water every living creature. Some of them crawl on their bellies, some walk on two legs, and some walk on four. Allah creates whatever He wills. Surely Allah is Most Capable of everything.
We have indeed sent down revelations clarifying ňĻthe truthňļ. But Allah ňĻonlyňļ guides whoever He wills to the Straight Path.
And the hypocrites say, ‚ÄúWe believe in Allah and the Messenger, and we obey.‚ÄĚ Then a group of them turns away soon after that. These are not ňĻtrueňļ believers.
And as soon as they are called to Allah and His Messenger so he may judge between them, a group of them <sup foot_note=715>715</sup> turns away.
But if the truth is in their favour, they come to him, fully submitting.
Is there a sickness in their hearts? Or are they in doubt? Or do they fear that Allah and His Messenger will be unjust to them? In fact, it is they who are the ňĻtrueňļ wrongdoers.
The only response of the ňĻtrueňļ believers, when they are called to Allah and His Messenger so he may judge between them, is to say, ‚ÄúWe hear and obey.‚ÄĚ It is they who will ňĻtrulyňļ succeed.
For whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, and fears Allah and is mindful of Him, then it is they who will ňĻtrulyňļ triumph.

They swear by Allah their most solemn oaths that if you ňĻO Prophetňļ were to command them, they would certainly march forth ňĻin Allah‚Äôs causeňļ. Say, ‚ÄúňĻYouňļ do not ňĻhave toňļ swear; your obedience is well known!‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=716>716</sup> Surely Allah is All-Aware of what you do.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.716: This is used in a sarcastic way. It means, ‚ÄúSave your oaths! Everyone knows you are only bluffing. It is actions that matter, not words.‚ÄĚ

text: Say, ‚ÄúObey Allah and obey the Messenger. But if you turn away, then he is only responsible for his duty <sup foot_note=717>717</sup> and you are responsible for yours. <sup foot_note=718>718</sup> And if you obey him, you will be ňĻrightlyňļ guided. The Messenger‚Äôs duty is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ clearly.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.717: The Prophet‚Äôs only duty is to deliver the message.
foot_notes.718: Their only duty is to obey the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) by submitting to Allah.

text: Allah has promised those of you who believe and do good that He will certainly make them successors in the land, as He did with those before them; and will surely establish for them their faith which He has chosen for them; and will indeed change their fear into security‚ÄĒňĻprovided thatňļ they worship Me, associating nothing with Me. But whoever disbelieves after this ňĻpromiseňļ, it is they who will be the rebellious.
Moreover, establish prayer, pay alms-tax, and obey the Messenger, so you may be shown mercy.
Do not think ňĻO Prophetňļ that the disbelievers can escape in the land. The Fire will be their home. Indeed, what an evil destination!

O believers! Let those ňĻbondspeopleňļ in your possession and those of you who are still under age ask for your permission ňĻto come inňļ at three times: before dawn prayer, when you take off your ňĻouterňļ clothes at noon, and after the late evening prayer. ňĻThese areňļ three times of privacy for you. Other than these times, there is no blame on you or them to move freely, attending to one another. This is how Allah makes the revelations clear to you, for Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

And when your children reach the age of puberty, let them seek permission ňĻto come inňļ, as their seniors do. This is how Allah makes His revelations clear to you, for Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

As for elderly women past the age of marriage, there is no blame on them if they take off their ňĻouterňļ garments, without revealing their adornments. But it is better for them if they avoid this ňĻaltogetherňļ. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

There is no restriction on the blind, or the disabled, or the sick. <sup foot_note=719>719</sup> Nor on yourselves if you eat from your homes, <sup foot_note=720>720</sup> or the homes of your fathers, or your mothers, or your brothers, or your sisters, or your paternal uncles, or your paternal aunts, or your maternal uncles, or your maternal aunts, or from the homes in your trust, or ňĻthe homes ofňļ your friends. There is no blame on you eating together or separately. However, when you enter houses, greet one another with a greeting ňĻof peaceňļ from Allah, blessed and good. <sup foot_note=721>721</sup> This is how Allah makes His revelations clear to you, so perhaps you will understand.

foot_notes.719: There is no blame on any of the three if they do not march forth in Allah‚Äôs cause. Moreover, some Muslims would give the keys of their homes to one of those who could not march forth (namely the blind, the disabled, or the sick) or their own relatives and ask them to enter their houses and eat at will but these people were shy to do that.
foot_notes.720: i.e., the home of your spouse or children.
foot_notes.721: If someone is in the house, greet them by saying, ‚Äė As-sal√Ęmu ‚Äô alaikum‚Äô (peace be upon you).‚ÄĚ But if there is no one there, you greet yourselves by saying, ‚Äė Asal√Ęmu ‚Äô alaina wa ‚Äô ala ‚Äô ib√Ęd-ill√Ęhi aŠĻ£-ŠĻ£√ĘliŠł•√ģn‚Äô (peace be upon us and all righteous servants of Allah).‚ÄĚ

text: The ňĻtrueňļ believers are only those who believe in Allah and His Messenger, and when they are with him on a public matter, they do not leave without his permission. Indeed, those who ask your permission ňĻO Prophetňļ are the ones who ňĻtrulyňļ believe in Allah and His Messenger. So when they ask your permission for a private matter, grant permission to whoever you wish and ask Allah‚Äôs forgiveness for them. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

Do not treat the Messenger‚Äôs summons to you ňĻas lightlyňļ as your summons to one another. <sup foot_note=722>722</sup> Allah certainly knows those of you who slip away, hiding behind others. <sup foot_note=723>723</sup> So let those who disobey his orders beware, for an affliction may befall them, or a painful torment may overtake them.

foot_notes.722: There is also a different interpretation: ‚ÄúDo not call the Messenger ňĻby his nameňļ as you call one another.‚ÄĚ In other words, Do not say, ‚ÄėO MuŠł•ammad.‚Äô Rather, say, ‚ÄėO Prophet.‚Äô
foot_notes.723: Some hypocrites used to sneak out while the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) was talking.

text: Surely to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. He knows well what you stand for. And ňĻonňļ the Day all will be returned to Him, He will inform them of what they did. For Allah has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.
